##NAMES=Gerstmannite
##RRUFFID=R130646
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Mn^2+^MgZn(SiO_4_)(OH)_2_
##LOCALITY=Sterling mine, Sterling Hill, Ogdensburg, Franklin Mining District, Sussex County, New Jersey, USA
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Rock Currier
##DESCRIPTION=Sprays of pale-pink bunched prismatic crystals associated with white willemite, brown sphalerite,  and dark-brown to black franklinite
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R130646
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Mn_0.80_Mg_0.20_)Mg_1.00_Zn_1.03_Si_0.99_O_4_(OH_1.42_F_0.58_) ; Probed with R170038
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 8.178 b: 18.645 c: 6.253 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 90 volume: 953.4 crystal system: orthorhombic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
